channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: World Vision Kenya rolls out national plan to improve water and sanitation for children
item.description: World Vision Kenya has launched a new national strategy, 'Mapping the Blue Thread', which aims to provide direct water, sanitation, and hygiene services to over 2.27 million children in 18 counties by...

item.title: Doug Ford appoints Travis Kann as chief of staff to strengthen Canada-U.S. ties
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford has announced a change in his office leadership, with Patrick Sackville stepping down as chief of staff. He will be succeeded by Travis Kann, who currently serves as principa...

item.title: President Zardari emphasises Pakistan's security, sovereignty and regional peace in parliamentary address
item.description: On Monday, President Asif Ali Zardari addressed a joint sitting of Parliament amid strong opposition sloganeering, reaffirming Pakistan's stance on national security and regional stability. He stresse...

item.title: Haryana CM Nayab Saini’s Saffron Turban Sparks Political Debate Ahead Of Punjab Elections
item.description: Haryana Chief Minister Nayab Singh Saini grabbed headlines not just for presenting the state budget but also for his choice of attire. During the budget session on Monday, Saini appeared wearing a bri...

item.title: Philippine Coast Guard Spokesperson Jay Tarriela Elevated to Rear Admiral
item.description: MANILA, Philippines — President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. has officially promoted Jay Tarriela, the Philippine Coast Guard (PCG) spokesperson for the West Philippine Sea, to the rank of rear admiral. This ...

item.title: Suspected Rs 590-crore discrepancy at IDFC First Bank Chandigarh branch raises operational concerns
item.description: In a major operational oversight, a Chandigarh branch of IDFC First Bank is under scrutiny for a suspected Rs 590-crore fraud. The issue came to light during routine year-end government reconciliation...

item.title: Murang'a County leads in job creation and service automation among Kenyan counties
item.description: Murang'a County has emerged top in a recent ranking on job creation and automation of services, according to a report by the Vision 2030 Delivery Secretariat. The assessment reviewed all 47 counties o...

item.title: Tanzania Launches FAO-Backed Pilot to Improve Access to Finance for Forest and Farm Producers
item.description: Tanzania has launched the Capitalisation of Financial Instruments (CFI) Pilot Project in Arusha as a new step to strengthen financing for forest and landscape restoration. The initiative is led by the...

item.title: Review finds Nationals’ nuclear power stance cost support in 2025 Federal election
item.description: A confidential review into the Nationals’ performance in the 2025 Federal election has revealed that the party’s focus on nuclear power hurt its electoral prospects. While voters were primarily concer...

item.title: US Tourist Injured by Shotgun at Bayug Falls in Bukidnon
item.description: A 38-year-old American tourist, identified only as Juven, was injured by a shotgun bullet while taking photos at Bayug Falls in Bukidnon, the Philippines’ second highest waterfall, on Sunday. Accordin...

item.title: Kenyan Government Airlifts Bodies of MP Ng'eno and Others After Nandi Helicopter Crash
item.description: On 2nd March 2026, the Kenyan government facilitated the airlifting of the remains of Emurua Dikirr MP Johanna Ng'eno and five others who perished in a fatal helicopter crash in Nandi County. The Euro...

item.title: FCT Minister Wike Reflects on Challenges and Support During Local Government Elections
item.description: The Minister of the Federal Capital Territory (FCT), Nyesom Wike, yan say plenty people wan make am fail for the local government elections wey just happen for Abuja. For him monthly media chat, Wike ...

item.title: Kenya’s counties showing progress in job creation and healthcare under devolution
item.description: A recent report by the Kenya Vision 2030 Delivery Secretariat has ranked Murang’a, Nakuru, Kiambu, and Kisumu as the top counties in Kenya for job creation, automation, and e-government services. Mura...

item.title: LTFRB launches Komyu-Konek program for reporting PUV violations
item.description: The Land Transportation Franchising and Regulatory Board (LTFRB) has officially launched the 'Komyu-Konek' program to encourage commuters to report any abuses committed by public utility vehicle (PUV)...

item.title: GPS and Navigation Systems Disrupted for Over 1,100 Ships Amid Middle East Conflict
item.description: Since the start of the United States and Israel’s military operations against Iran on February 28, 2026, over 1,100 ships in the Middle East have experienced GPS and AIS (Automatic Identification Syst...

item.title: How to set Mail & Guardian as your preferred news source on Google
item.description: Google has introduced a new feature that allows users to personalise their news experience by selecting preferred publications for the 'top stories' section in search results. This means you no longer...

item.title: Trump criticised for aggressive actions in Iran amid UK political reactions
item.description: The article critiques former US President Donald Trump's recent actions following his omission from the Nobel Peace Prize. It highlights Trump's pivot from peace initiatives to military actions, inclu...

item.title: Presidency Raises Concern Over ADC’s Digital Membership Registration Platform
item.description: The Senior Special Assistant on Digital and New Media to President Bola Tinubu, O’tega Ogra, don tok say the new online membership registration system of the African Democratic Congress (ADC) get waha...

item.title: Inside Trump’s Decision to Launch Military Action Against Iran
item.description: In early 2026, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu pressed US President Donald Trump for approval to strike Iran’s missile sites. Over months of secret discussions, Trump administration official...

item.title: University of Karachi halts academic activities in solidarity with victims of Iran attacks
item.description: The University of Karachi (KU) has suspended all classes and examinations scheduled for March 2 in order to express solidarity with the victims of the recent attacks in Iran. According to an official ...
